symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread intensely pruritic wheals with a migratory pattern

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pruritic wheals are the defining feature of urticaria.

symptoms.symptom_name: Individual lesions transient (<24 hours) and migratory — appear and resolve within hours while new lesions erupt elsewhere

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Transient migratory wheals each lasting <24h are essentially diagnostic of urticaria.
EM target lesions are fixed and persist for days; transient migration excludes EM.

symptoms.symptom_name: Wheals with central pallor, surrounding erythematous flare, and blanching on pressure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Central pallor with an erythematous flare and blanching are typical of dermal wheals; the appearance is video-observable in telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent ingestion of a new NSAID (ibuprofen) approximately 12 hours before onset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: NSAIDs are a common pseudoallergic precipitant of acute urticaria via COX-1 inhibition; temporal association supports a drug trigger and directs management (cease NSAID).

symptoms.symptom_name: No airway involvement — no lip/tongue/throat swelling, no stridor, no wheeze, no dysphagia; speaking in full sentences

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of airway/mucosal involvement is required to safely exclude anaphylaxis and permit a low baseline tier.

symptoms.symptom_name: No circulatory or gastrointestinal involvement — no dizziness, faintness, vomiting or abdominal cramps

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of a second (circulatory/GI) ASCIA limb confirms the reaction is isolated cutaneous urticaria, not systemic anaphylaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No blistering, no mucosal (mouth/eye/genital) lesions, no skin pain, no fever or systemic upset

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fixed target lesions, blistering, mucosal involvement and systemic upset argues against a severe cutaneous drug reaction (EM major / SJS-TEN).
Absence of scaling and vesicles, with a generalised migratory rather than fixed exposure-pattern rash, argues against contact dermatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs and clear oropharynx on clinician examination (BP/HR/RR/SpO2/temperature normal; no uvular oedema)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective normal haemodynamics and a clear airway (sealed clinician measurement) confirm no systemic reaction; the AI Doctor infers airway/circulatory safety from history and video, not from measured vitals.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Intensely pruritic wheals + transient migratory behaviour + central pallor with blanching together form a video-recognisable urticaria constellation that is diagnostic on appearance and history alone in telehealth.
The combined absence of airway and circulatory/GI involvement is what safely excludes anaphylaxis and licenses the low baseline tier — while the safety net for future evolution is retained.
